signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Tension + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.66 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid as Middle East tensions escalate following a +4.39% surge in WTI crude. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains crowded, leaving the pair vulnerable to sharp unwinds if VIX exceeds 25. ING notes diminishing impact of BoJ intervention, yet 160.00 remains the critical threshold for potential MoF action. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 17.5 (normal volatility).

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike + Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7197 — RBA raised the cash rate to 4.35% (8-1 vote), creating a -60bp carry deficit for USD. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) shows institutional conviction, while retail sentiment is 82% short. This institutional vs retail divergence at the 0.7200 handle suggests retail capitulation risk if buyers maintain technical control above the 100/200 hour MA. Upside momentum is supported by the third consecutive RBA hike.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Positioning
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1710 — Correlation with EuroStoxx has shifted to +0.731 (z=+1.86σ), indicating EUR is trading as a pure risk-proxy rather than on rate differentials. COT LF net short -18,293 (45.4% long) shows specs are fading the recent 1M +1.45% rally. Rabobank notes upside is structurally limited by the 175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage. The bull flattener regime (2s10s +53bp) suggests the market is still pricing a restrictive Fed stance.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5902 — COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) shows specs are heavily positioned against the NZD despite a hawkish RBNZ bias (Market bias: Hike). This positioning-policy divergence creates short-squeeze risk heading into the 27 May RBNZ meeting. USD/NZD 150bp carry advantage currently protects the USD side, but a hawkish delivery from RBNZ targets a move toward 0.6000.
